summary: Matched and heavily expanded src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c while renaming many VS Records diagram functions and related game-mode persistent data types. The PR converted numerous address-based or generic symbols into descriptive mnDiagram_ names, added/updated headers and symbols, introduced clearer persistent fighter/name-tag structs, and propagated those types through gmmain_lib and menu users. Review focused on enforcing module-prefixed public names, moving user-data structs into mn/types.h, keeping definitions/linkage-friendly at the top or in .static.h, and recognizing an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.
what_changed: Expanded mndiagram.c from a small set of stubs/address-named functions into a much larger implementation slice, with the PR context reporting 2158 added and 47 deleted lines in that file.

Renamed many mndiagram symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt from address-based names or generic fn_ names to descriptive public names such as mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex, mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex, mnDiagram_IsDistanceOverflow, mnDiagram_ConvertDistanceForDisplay, mnDiagram_GetHitPercentage, mnDiagram_FormatTime, mnDiagram_InputProc, mnDiagram_OnFrame, and mnDiagram_CursorProc.

Renamed generic mndiagram statistic helpers from unprefixed names like GetHitPercentage, GetPlayPercentage, GetAveragePlayerCount, GetNameTotalKOs, GetFighterTotalFalls to module-prefixed mnDiagram_ equivalents.
Renamed menu helper symbols outside mndiagram, including mn_8022E950 to mn_IsFighterUnlocked, mn_8022EB24 to mn_GetDigitAt, and mn_8022EB78 to mn_GetDigitCount.

Added or adjusted mndiagram helper logic for sorted fighter/name lookup, distance display overflow, distance conversion, hit/play percentages, KO/fall totals, number/time formatting, cursor navigation, ranked fighter lookup, popup/input/on-frame callbacks, and scroll-arrow visibility based on the symbol and diff evidence.

Refined game-mode persistent data types: GetPersistentFighterData now returns struct FighterData*, GetPersistentNameData now returns struct NameTagData*, and gmmain_lib.c call sites changed from gmm_x1F2C and gmm_x2FF8_inner/gmm_x2FF8 to FighterData, NameTagData, and NameTagDataBank.

Flattened or clarified the GameRules initializer in gmmain_lib.c by replacing nested anonymous initializer blocks with named/commented scalar fields such as unk_xc, xD, xE, xF, unk_x10, x11, x12, and x13.
Added mn-side type/header support, including changes to mn/types.h, mndiagram.h, mndiagram.static.h, mndiagram2.h, mndiagram3.h, forward.h, and related mnmain/mnname headers.

The author noted the work was based on Dolphin debugging, mostly produced with Claude then manually cleaned up, with some functions matched unassisted; the author also acknowledged broken/regressed matches and planned to fix them.
smart_moves: Converted symbol-table names at the same time as source/header prototypes, making the renamed mndiagram functions searchable and keeping config/GALE01/symbols.txt aligned with decompiled code.
Used descriptive names that encode observed behavior rather than just addresses, especially for statistics helpers, popup/input procs, cursor procs, and distance-formatting helpers.
Propagated persistent data type improvements through gm code instead of leaving mndiagram to cast anonymous or address-derived structs locally.
Added size-checked structs for inferred layouts in the mndiagram area according to the review excerpt, using STATIC_ASSERT to guard layout assumptions.
Asked for naming guidance during review and applied the project convention that non-static functions should carry the module prefix.
Identified a likely inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility, which is a useful matching clue for nearby control-flow and call-site cleanup.
decomp_lessons: For public/non-static functions in a module, use the module prefix consistently; in this PR the reviewer explicitly said all non-static mndiagram functions should be prefixed with mnDiagram_.
When a callback or GObj user-data blob has a stable layout, promote it into a named type in the appropriate module types header rather than keeping ad hoc local typedefs or casts in a C file.

For decomp matching in UI/menu files, descriptive callback names such as InputProc, PopupInputProc, PopupCleanup, PopupAnimProc, OnFrame, CursorProc, and ExitAnimProc can document lifecycle roles while preserving address mapping in symbols.txt.
Keep definitions needed for linking near the top of the file or in the module .static.h; review noted this makes linking easier.
If a function body appears duplicated at a call site, consider whether the original had an inline helper; the review noted code looked like an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.
Struct renaming in core persistent-data areas should be propagated to accessor return types and all call sites to reduce void* usage and improve later decomp readability.
Debugging in Dolphin can inform semantic renames, but AI-generated or partially matched functions still need manual cleanup and match-regression checks.
naming_conventions: All non-static mndiagram functions should use the module prefix mnDiagram_.
Previously unprefixed helpers like GetHitPercentage and GetPlayPercentage were renamed to mnDiagram_GetHitPercentage and mnDiagram_GetPlayPercentage.
Address-based mndiagram names were replaced with behavior names where evidence was strong, e.g. mnDiagram_8023EA2C to mnDiagram_GetFighterByIndex and mnDiagram_8023EA40 to mnDiagram_GetNameByIndex.
Generic fn_ callback symbols were renamed with role suffixes such as Proc, Cleanup, OnFrame, and CursorProc.
Game-mode persistent data structs were renamed away from offset-derived names toward semantic names: gmm_x1F2C to FighterData, gmm_x2FF8_inner to NameTagData, and gmm_x2FF8 to NameTagDataBank.
Menu helper functions outside mndiagram used the mn_ prefix with descriptive verbs, e.g. mn_IsFighterUnlocked, mn_GetDigitAt, and mn_GetDigitCount.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Preserved exact address mappings and sizes in config/GALE01/symbols.txt while renaming functions, allowing semantic source names without losing binary correspondence.
Used inferred structs with explicit offsets and STATIC_ASSERT size checks for mndiagram data/assets layouts, reducing fragile raw-offset access.
Moved toward typed accessors for persistent fighter and name data, which can make pointer arithmetic and array indexing easier to match and reason about.
Review suggested placing definitions at the top of the file or in .static.h to simplify linking.
Review identified a likely inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility, indicating that future matching should test inline extraction rather than forcing duplicated standalone code.
The PR body says several unmatched functions were still only 70-90% complete, so the exact matching tactics for those bodies are only partially evidenced in the provided slice.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Asked whether newly named functions should all be prefixed, noting inconsistent use of mn_ or existing names.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: All non-static functions should be prefixed with the module name, mnDiagram_.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The user data should be a new type in mn/types.h, possibly called Diagram for now.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: When linking, definitions are easier to handle if placed at the top of the file or in .static.h.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: A code region looked like an inline of mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility.

review_feedback.author: itsgrimetime
review_feedback.feedback: Acknowledged broken and regressed matches and said they would fix them.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching src/melee/mn/mndiagram.c after PR 2125 to see whether the remaining 70-90% unmatched functions were completed.
Search for mnDiagram_UpdateScrollArrowVisibility to verify where it was inlined and whether later decomp work extracted or renamed related call sites.
Search for struct Diagram or mn Diagram user-data types in mn/types.h to confirm how the reviewer suggestion was finalized.
Search for FighterData, NameTagData, and NameTagDataBank to see how persistent game-mode structs evolved after this rename.
Review later symbols.txt changes around 0x8023EA2C-0x802437E8 for additional mndiagram semantic renames.
